decks.notes: The deck revolves around utilizing cards like Opt, Peek, and Portent to dig through the deck quickly in order to find combo pieces like Phyrexian Dreadnought and Vision Charm. By combining these cards with counterspells like Counterspell and Foil, the deck aims to protect its key combo pieces while also disrupting the opponent's plays with Stifle and Thwart. Ultimately, the goal is to assemble the combo and unleash it quickly, overwhelming the opponent before they have a chance to respond effectively.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly flood the board with aggressive Goblin creatures and overwhelm the opponent with their speed and synergy. Cards like Goblin Lackey and Goblin Matron help to quickly search for key Goblin cards, while Goblin Warchief and Goblin King provide boosts to all Goblins on the battlefield. Gempalm Incinerator and Siege-Gang Commander offer removal options and additional damage, making it a fast and efficient aggro deck that focuses on building a strong board presence and attacking relentlessly.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly get out Phyrexian Dreadnought and Hermit Druid, then use various spells to either reanimate Dreadnought for a massive creature, or combo off with Hermit Druid to fill the graveyard and win the game. The deck aims to use its fast mana sources and cheap spells to disrupt the opponent while assembling its combo pieces, ultimately winning the game through a powerful graveyard strategy.

This deck focuses on using control elements and card draw to steadily grow in power and gain control of the game. It utilizes efficient counterspells and removal spells to control the board while leveraging card draw spells like Accumulated Knowledge and Gush to draw into key pieces. Psychatog and Quirion Dryad serve as win conditions that grow stronger as the game progresses. The deck also includes disruption elements like Meddling Mage and disruption spells like Foil to disrupt the opponent's game plan.

This deck focuses on quickly ramping up mana with cards like Gaea's Cradle and Priest of Titania to swarm the board with Elves and overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures. The key strategy is to utilize cards like Wirewood Symbiote and Survival of the Fittest to constantly replenish the hand and keep the pressure on the opponent. By efficiently using mana acceleration and card draw, this Aggro-Elves deck aims to establish a strong board presence early in the game and maintain momentum to secure a quick victory.

This deck focuses on playing enchantments that provide card advantage and mana ramp, using cards like Argothian Enchantress, Mirri's Guile, and Enchantress's Presence. The goal is to assemble a combination of cards like Opalescence and Serra's Sanctum to create a powerful board state that can overwhelm the opponent quickly. The deck also includes removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Seal of Cleansing to deal with threats. Overall, the deck aims to create a strong enchantress engine that can lead to explosive plays and secure victory.

The deck focuses on quickly filling up the graveyard with enchantments, then using Replenish to bring them all back into play at once for a game-winning combo. It includes cards like Opalescence and Parallax Wave to create powerful board states and control the opponent's board. The deck also has disruption and card draw to help set up the combo and protect it once it's assembled. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with powerful enchantments and lock them out of the game while setting up a win condition.

The main idea of this deck is to lock down the opponent's ability to untap their lands by using cards like Stasis and Forsaken City. This allows the player to control the pace of the game and eventually win through other means, such as using Black Vise to chip away at the opponent's life total or setting up a big combo finish with cards like Brain Freeze. The deck also includes a number of counterspells and card draw spells to help protect the combo pieces and dig for key cards.

This deck focuses on aggressive play with Goblins as the main creature type. Utilizing cards like Goblin Lackey to cheat out creatures early, as well as powerful Goblin lords like Goblin Warchief to buff the team, the goal is to overwhelm the opponent quickly before they can stabilize. Cards like Goblin Ringleader help draw into more threats, while cards like Gempalm Incinerator offer removal options. The deck also includes utility cards like Naturalize for dealing with problematic artifacts or enchantments. Overall, the strategy is to flood the board with Goblins and beat down the opponent efficiently.

The core strategy of this deck revolves around generating mana quickly and efficiently, chaining together spells like Frantic Search and Meditate to draw through the deck, eventually leading to a massive Brain Freeze finisher. The deck also utilizes Cunning Wish to tutor for specific combo pieces or disruption, while cards like Lion's Eye Diamond and Helm of Awakening enable explosive turns. The Mono Blue Storm deck aims to win the game by casting a large number of spells in a single turn, overwhelming the opponent with a barrage of spells and eventually milling them out with Brain Freeze.

This Elf aggro deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a fast and aggressive creature strategy. By utilizing mana acceleration from cards like Llanowar Elves and Priest of Titania, the deck can quickly ramp into powerful threats like Deranged Hermit and Multani's Acolyte. Survival of the Fittest provides card advantage and ensures the deck has the right creatures at the right time. Gaea's Cradle further boosts mana production, allowing for explosive turns that can quickly close out the game before opponents have a chance to stabilize.

The main idea of this deck is to utilize the combo between Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle to quickly bring out a powerful threat that can overwhelm the opponent. The deck includes a variety of card draw spells and counterspells to protect the combo and disrupt the opponent's strategy. It also has utility cards like Portent and Vision Charm to manipulate the top of the deck and control the flow of the game. By efficiently managing resources and timing plays, the deck aims to outmaneuver the opponent and secure victory through an explosive combo.

The deck focuses on utilizing Survival of the Fittest to efficiently tutor for key creatures and combo pieces while disrupting the opponent's game plan with control elements like Mystic Snake and Opposition. The deck aims to generate value by constantly recurring creatures like Basking Rootwalla and Wall of Blossoms, while also ramping up mana quickly with Gaea's Cradle and mana dorks like Llanowar Elves. The inclusion of cards like Natural Order and Deranged Hermit provides a powerful finisher to overwhelm the opponent and secure victory through creature advantage and control tactics.

This deck aims to quickly assemble the Phyrexian Devourer and Fling combo, using Tinker to search for the needed pieces. The Altar of Dementia helps fuel the combo by sacrificing creatures to mill cards. The deck includes various mana accelerants and card draw spells to consistently find the combo pieces. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a massive amount of damage in a single turn, potentially winning the game out of nowhere. Defensive spells like Defense Grid help protect the combo while setting up for a lethal Fling.

This deck focuses on aggressive play with a focus on Goblin creatures, utilizing cards like Goblin Warchief, Goblin Piledriver, and Goblin Ringleader to quickly overwhelm the opponent. It includes utility cards like Naturalize to deal with opposing threats and disrupt their game plan. The deck also features land destruction with cards like Rishadan Port and Wasteland to further hinder the opponent's strategy. Overall, the key strategy of this deck is to go wide with Goblin creatures and apply pressure early in the game to secure a victory.

The deck focuses on quickly assembling the combo of Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle to create a powerful threat that can take opponents by surprise. The deck utilizes card draw and counter spells to protect the combo pieces and disrupt opponent's strategies. The goal is to control the game tempo and win through a fast and efficient combo.
